ConstructorArguments: Move to a new position. Unlike Transform, if you have `UseController` enabled, this will sweep the shadow to the new position, rather than teleporting there.

Documentation.Summary:
Move to a new position. Unlike Transform, if you have `UseController` enabled, this will sweep the shadow
to the new position, rather than teleporting there.
Add a sphere shape to this body.
Documentation.Return: The newly created shape, if any.

Params.center: Center of the sphere, relative to <see cref="P:Sandbox.PhysicsBody.Position" /> of this body.
Params.rebuildMass: Whether the mass should be <see cref="M:Sandbox.PhysicsBody.RebuildMass">recalculated</see> after adding the shape.

ConstructorArguments: Add a sphere shape to this body.

Documentation.Summary: Add a sphere shape to this body.
Add a capsule shape to this body.
Documentation.Return: The newly created shape, or null on failure.

Params.center: Point A of the capsule, relative to <see cref="P:Sandbox.PhysicsBody.Position" /> of this body.
Params.center2: Point B of the capsule, relative to <see cref="P:Sandbox.PhysicsBody.Position" /> of this body.
Params.radius: Radius of the capsule end caps.
Params.rebuildMass: Whether the mass should be <see cref="M:Sandbox.PhysicsBody.RebuildMass">recalculated</see> after adding the shape.

Documentation.Summary: Add a box shape to this body.
Documentation.Return: The newly created shape, or null on failure.

Params.position: Center of the box, relative to <see cref="P:Sandbox.PhysicsBody.Position" /> of this body.
Params.rotation: Rotation of the box, relative to <see cref="P:Sandbox.PhysicsBody.Rotation" /> of this body.
Params.extent: The extents of the box. The box will extend from its center by this much in both negative and positive directions of each axis.
Params.rebuildMass: Whether the mass should be <see cref="M:Sandbox.PhysicsBody.RebuildMass">recalculated</see> after adding the shape.

Remove all physics shapes, but not the physics body itself.

Documentation.Summary: Remove all physics shapes, but not the physics body itself.

ConstructorArguments: Meant to be only used on dynamic bodies, rebuilds mass from all shapes of this body based on their volume and PhysicsBody.Surface, for cases where they may have changed.

Documentation.Summary: Meant to be only used on dynamic bodies, rebuilds mass from all shapes of this body based on their volume and physics properties, for cases where they may have changed.
Completely removes this physics body.

ConstructorArguments: Applies instant linear impulse (i.e. a bullet impact) to this body at its center of mass. For continuous force (i.e. a moving car), use PhysicsBody.ApplyForce(Vector3)

Documentation.Summary:
Applies instant linear impulse (i.e. a bullet impact) to this body at its center of mass.
For continuous force (i.e. a moving car), use <see cref="M:Sandbox.PhysicsBody.ApplyForce(Vector3)" />

ConstructorArguments: Applies instant linear impulse (i.e. a bullet impact) to this body at given position. For continuous force (i.e. a moving car), use PhysicsBody.ApplyForceAt(Vector3,Vector3)

Documentation.Summary:
Applies instant linear impulse (i.e. a bullet impact) to this body at given position.
For continuous force (i.e. a moving car), use <see cref="M:Sandbox.PhysicsBody.ApplyForceAt(Vector3,Vector3)" />

ConstructorArguments: Applies instant angular impulse (i.e. a bullet impact) to this body. For continuous force (i.e. a moving car), use PhysicsBody.ApplyTorque(Vector3)

Applies instant angular impulse (i.e. a bullet impact) to this body.
For continuous force (i.e. a moving car), use <see cref="M:Sandbox.PhysicsBody.ApplyTorque(Vector3)" />

Applies force to this body at the center of mass.

ConstructorArguments: If true we'll create a controller for this physics body. This is useful for keyframed physics objects that need to push things. The controller will sweep as the entity moves, rather than teleporting the object.. which works better when pushing dynamic objects etc.

Documentation.Summary:
If true we'll create a controller for this physics body. This is useful
for keyframed physics objects that need to push things. The controller will
sweep as the entity moves, rather than teleporting the object.. which works better
when pushing dynamic objects etc.

ConstructorArguments: Whether this body is allowed to automatically go into "sleep" after a certain amount of time of inactivity. PhysicsBody.Sleeping for more info on the sleep mechanic.

Whether this body is allowed to automatically go into "sleep" after a certain amount of time of inactivity.

ConstructorArguments: The speed threshold below which this body will be put to sleep. Units per second. The default is about 2 units/sec. Increase this to make bodies sleep sooner, which is useful for stacking stability.

Documentation.Summary:
The speed threshold below which this body will be put to sleep. Units per second.
The default is about 2 units/sec. Increase this to make bodies sleep sooner, which is useful for stacking stability.

ConstructorArguments: Enable enhanced continuous collision detection (CCD) for this body. When enabled, the body performs CCD against dynamic bodies (but not against other bodies with enhanced CCD enabled). This is useful for fast-moving objects like bullets or rockets that need reliable collision detection.

Documentation.Summary:
Enable enhanced continuous collision detection (CCD) for this body.
When enabled, the body performs CCD against dynamic bodies
(but not against other bodies with enhanced CCD enabled).
This is useful for fast-moving objects like bullets or rockets
that need reliable collision detection.

ConstructorArguments: Represents a physics object. An entity can have multiple physics objects. See PhysicsBody.PhysicsGroup. A physics objects consists of one or more PhysicsShapes.
Represents a physics object. An entity can have multiple physics objects. See PhysicsBody.PhysicsGroup. A physics objects consists of one or more PhysicsShapes.

Documentation.Summary:
Represents a physics object. An entity can have multiple physics objects. See <see cref="P:Sandbox.PhysicsBody.PhysicsGroup">PhysicsGroup</see>.
A physics objects consists of one or more <see cref="T:Sandbox.PhysicsShape">PhysicsShape</see>s.
